statement: Passing an automated accessibility scanner with zero errors proves a product is accessible.
why_it_spreads: Automated tools are fast, cheap and produce a reassuring green pass/fail, which is mistaken for full coverage and treated as legal cover.
evidence_against: WebAIM (src-webaim): automated tools detect only around 40% of WCAG failures and absence of detected errors does not indicate conformance.
W3C WAI (src-w3c-wai) and WCAG (src-wcag22): many success criteria require human judgement (meaningful alt text, focus order, reading level, context).
Assistive-technology and keyboard testing routinely surface barriers no scanner reports.
replacement_principle: Treat automated scanning as one input in a layered process that also includes manual keyboard testing, screen-reader review and assessment against WCAG by a knowledgeable person.
